literal_meaning: that taṇhā which leads to renewed becoming
preferred_translation: that ignorant wanting that leads to renewed becoming
alternative_translations: that craving that leads to renewed becoming
definition: Opening segment of the classic four-truth definition of taṇhā.
notes: Use this record when the stock formula is translated as a unit. Keep taṇhā distinct from generic desire-language and preserve the renewed-becoming force of ponobbhavikā.

example_phrases.translation: that ignorant wanting that leads to renewed becoming, accompanied by relishing and passion, delighting here and there
